message:
docs: record statfs group-descriptor aggregation + JBD2 tag layout details

Two documentation updates that catch the spec + parity surface up
to the ffs-core / ffs-journal behaviour change landing alongside.
No schema or code change, and no previously-frozen test contract
is relaxed — the docs just track current reality.

`COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md`:

- **§11.6.3 JBD2 tag layout.** Add a paragraph making the exact
  non-CSUM_V3 tag shape explicit: the base layout is `{ t_blocknr:
  u32, t_checksum: u16, t_flags: u16 }` plus `t_blocknr_high: u32`
  when the journal advertises 64-bit block numbers. The existing
  SAME_UUID carve-out (16-byte UUID follows when the flag is clear)
  now appears alongside this text, with an explicit call-out that
  the trailing UUID must be accounted for before locating the next
  tag. This closes an implicit ambiguity that the adjacent
  ffs-journal commit surfaces by adding real CSUM_V2 / CSUM_V3
  handling — anyone reading the spec while reading that code now
  gets the exact byte layout in one place.

- **§13.1 statfs table.** Two columns flip:
    - `f_bfree`: was documented as `s_free_blocks_count_lo/hi`;
      updated to "Sum of `bg_free_blocks_count` across all groups".
    - `f_ffree`: was `s_free_inodes_count`; updated to "Sum of
      `bg_free_inodes_count` across all groups".
  Motivation line added one paragraph up: "from superblock geometry
  and aggregated group descriptors". The MVCC-snapshot caveat
  beneath the table (free counts under a snapshot may be slightly
  stale) is unchanged; aggregation over group descriptors does not
  bypass that freshness boundary.

`FEATURE_PARITY.md`:

- New paragraph in the §2 mounted-path ext4 parity section freezes
  the statfs GD-aggregation contract explicitly: total geometry
  still comes from the superblock, free block and inode counts are
  aggregated from live group descriptors so write-side allocation
  changes are visible to `statfs` without relying on stale
  open-time superblock counters. This is the new assertion the
  regression test `open_fs_fsops_statfs_ext4_aggregates_group_
  descriptor_counts` exercises in ffs-core.

Both files are hand-written top-to-bottom; no autogeneration
involved, so the diff is exactly the intentional prose change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3722,6 +3722,11 @@ All blocks start with 12-byte header: `{ h_magic: 0xC03B3998, h_blocktype, h_seq
 
 16 bytes per tag. If `!SAME_UUID`: 16-byte UUID follows.
 
+For non-`CSUM_V3` descriptor tags, the base layout is `{ t_blocknr: u32,
+t_checksum: u16, t_flags: u16 }` plus `t_blocknr_high: u32` when the journal
+advertises 64-bit block numbers. The same `!SAME_UUID` rule applies: a 16-byte
+UUID follows the base tag and must be accounted for before the next tag.
+
 #### 11.6.4 Replay Algorithm
 
 1. **SCAN:** From `s_start`, seq=`s_sequence`. Match magic+seq. Parse DESCRIPTOR/COMMIT/REVOKE. Stop on mismatch.
@@ -4218,16 +4223,17 @@ inode-only; kernel handles actual I/O in memory.
 
 ### 13.1 statfs
 
-Returns filesystem statistics from superblock and aggregated group descriptors.
+Returns filesystem statistics from superblock geometry and aggregated group
+descriptors.
 
 | `statvfs` Field | Source |
 |----------------|--------|
 | `f_bsize` | `1024 << s_log_block_size` |
 | `f_blocks` | `s_blocks_count_lo/hi` |
-| `f_bfree` | `s_free_blocks_count_lo/hi` |
+| `f_bfree` | Sum of `bg_free_blocks_count` across all groups |
 | `f_bavail` | `f_bfree - s_r_blocks_count` (minus reserved-for-root) |
 | `f_files` | `s_inodes_count` |
-| `f_ffree` | `s_free_inodes_count` |
+| `f_ffree` | Sum of `bg_free_inodes_count` across all groups |
 | `f_namemax` | 255 |
 
 MVCC note: free counts read under a snapshot may be slightly stale; POSIX

@@ -186,6 +186,11 @@ source paths, with no visible workspace-entry or witness-file drift.
 Mounted-path ext4 symlink/refusal coverage now also freezes exact `EINVAL` for
 `readlink` on both regular-file and directory non-symlink paths, with no
 directory-entry or file-byte drift.
+FUSE/statfs parity now also freezes the ext4 group-descriptor accounting
+contract: total geometry still comes from the superblock, while free block and
+inode counts are aggregated from live group descriptors so write-side allocation
+changes are visible to `statfs` without relying on stale open-time superblock
+counters.
 
 ### 2.1 btrfs Experimental RW Capability Contract (Machine-Checkable)
